attributes.content:
## Overview

The Waiver / Release card gives the workspace a structured home for liability waiver with scope, parties, and acknowledgment. AI-generated language with model attribution.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Waiver / Release acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Waiver / Release can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Waiver / Release can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Signature]]** -- Signature can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Waiver / Release.
- **[[Alert]]** -- Alert can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Waiver / Release.
- **[[Report]]** -- Waiver / Release can feed rollups and summaries that surface trends, status, or outcomes.
- **[[Legal Contract]]** -- Legal Contract can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Waiver / Release.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Waiver / Release as the structured home for liability waiver with scope, parties, and acknowledgment. AI-generated language with model attribution and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Waiver / Release when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Waiver / Release stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
